text.title: Rails 7+ Backend Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Rails 7+ codebases. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: User receives welcome email; backend rolled the user creation back; user record is missing.
antipattern.root-cause: `UserMailer.with(...).welcome.deliver_later` fired inside `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction`; the job enqueued before commit.
antipattern.fix: Move dispatch to `after_create_commit` callback OR chain a follow-on Sidekiq job after the transaction returns success.

antipattern.symptom: Controllers handle three different result shapes (`ok:/value:`, `success:/data:`, `ok?/payload`); a refactor breaks one path silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Each service file defined its own `ServiceResult`-like class.
antipattern.fix: Lock a single canonical `ServiceResult` class. Add a CI grep that fails on additional `class ServiceResult` definitions.

antipattern.symptom: Job runs against stale record; user sees an old email subject; logs show `User#cached_email` mismatch.
antipattern.root-cause: Job declared `perform(user)` and was called with `UserMailer.deliver_later(user)`; YAML serialised the object at dispatch.
antipattern.fix: Change `perform(user_id)`; load fresh record inside `perform`. Use `User.find(user_id)` to surface 404 if the row vanished.

antipattern.symptom: Service mass-assigns `is_admin=1` because the controller passed raw `params`.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped `params.require(...).permit(...)` and handed the raw params hash to the service.
antipattern.fix: Restore Strong Params at the controller; pass only `permitted` hash to the service. Add a request spec that asserts protected attrs are dropped.

antipattern.symptom: Production deploy drops a column that the previous release still wrote to; immediate outage.
antipattern.root-cause: Migration contained `remove_column :users, :legacy_id` and shipped without a manual review.
antipattern.fix: Gate `remove_column` / `rename_column` PRs on a `MIGRATION-REVIEW.md` checklist; require an approver signed off explicitly in the PR description.
